evidence.scientific_clarity: Sankey flow correctly shows 1120→1000→820 split into Train/Val/Test/Reject with proportional widths and annotated counts; claim is fully legible.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 set; PDF uses Type3 outline fonts (not embedded TrueType), and SVG glyphs are path outlines not selectable text; default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Red (#CA0020) and green (#4DAC26, #1A9641) appear together — problematic for deuteranopia/protanopia; Raw (#2166AC) and Val (#0571B0) are near-identical blues; not colorblind-safe.
evidence.layout_density: 11×6 inch canvas is well-used; nodes span the full height; flow bands fill horizontal space; no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels observed.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption numbers match figure flows; legend is implicit (node labels with counts); no explicit legend box — a reviewer may ask for one; title duplicates caption intent.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF contains Type3 (outline/bitmap) fonts, not TrueType (fonttype=42 not set); text is not selectable/searchable in the PDF; SVG also uses path glyphs not text elements.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial (PDF 16 KB, PNG 58 KB).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states specific numbers (1120 raw, 1000 cleaned, 820 filtered, 600/120/100 splits, 180 total rejected) and explains flow-width encoding — strong quantitative claim.

overall_strengths: Clear, well-annotated Sankey with correct proportional flows and per-link count labels that match the caption's numerical claims exactly.
All required output files are present and non-trivial; caption is detailed and quantitatively precise.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 not set: PDF embeds Type3 outline fonts instead of TrueType, making text non-selectable and non-editable; SVG likewise uses path glyphs.
Palette is not colorblind-safe: red (#CA0020) paired with two greens (#1A9641, #4DAC26), and two near-identical blues (Raw vs Val) risk confusion for deuteranopes.
